root_text.sn36.17:1.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn36.17:1.2: “katamā nu kho, bhante, vedanā, katamo vedanāsamudayo, katamo vedanānirodho, katamā vedanānirodhagāminī paṭipadā?
root_text.sn36.17:1.3: Ko vedanāya assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇan”ti?
root_text.sn36.17:1.5: sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā—
root_text.sn36.17:1.9: Ayameva ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo vedanānirodhagāminī paṭipadā, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn36.17:1.11: Yaṁ vedanaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ vedanāya assādo.
root_text.sn36.17:1.12: Yaṁ vedanā aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammā, ayaṁ vedanāya ādīnavo.
root_text.sn36.17:1.13: Yo vedanāya chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ vedanāya nissaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn36.17:2.1: Atha kho pana, bhikkhave, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ nirodho akkhāto.
root_text.sn36.17:2.2: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā niruddhā hoti …pe…
root_text.sn36.17:2.3: khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo niruddho hoti, doso niruddho hoti, moho niruddho hoti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.4: Atha kho pana, bhikkhave, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto.
root_text.sn36.17:2.5: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā vūpasantā hoti …pe…
root_text.sn36.17:2.6: khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo vūpasanto hoti, doso vūpasanto hoti, moho vūpasanto hoti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.8: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā paṭippassaddhā hoti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.9: Dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vitakkavicārā paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.10: Tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa pīti paṭippassaddhā hoti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.11: Catutthaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa assāsapassāsā paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.12: Saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.17:2.13: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo paṭippassaddho hoti, doso paṭippassaddho hoti, moho paṭippassaddho hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn36.17:1.1: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn36.17:1.2: “Sir, what is feeling? What’s the origin of feeling? What’s the cessation of feeling? What’s the practice that leads to the cessation of feeling?
translation_text.sn36.17:1.3: And what is feeling’s gratification, drawback, and escape?”
translation_text.sn36.17:1.9: The practice that leads to the cessation of feelings is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn36.17:1.10: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn36.17:1.11: The pleasure and happiness that arise from feeling: this is its gratification.
translation_text.sn36.17:1.12: That feeling is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn36.17:1.13: Removing and giving up desire and greed for feeling: this is its escape.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.1: But I have also explained the progressive cessation of activities. …
translation_text.sn36.17:2.2: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has ceased. …
translation_text.sn36.17:2.3: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have ceased.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.4: And I have also explained the progressive settling of activities.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.5: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has stilled. …
translation_text.sn36.17:2.6: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have stilled.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.7: There are these six levels of tranquility.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.8: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.9: For someone who has attained the second absorption, the placing of the mind and keeping it connected have been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.10: For someone who has attained the third absorption, rapture has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.11: For someone who has attained the fourth absorption, breathing has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.12: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.17:2.13: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have been tranquilized.”
